---
title: "🇩🇪 German A1"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: none
    page-layout: full
    embed-resources: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Nunito:wght@400;600;700;800;900&family=Fira+Code:wght@400;500&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
        <style>
          /* ── Reset & Base ─────────────────────────── */
          *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
          body {
            font-family: 'Nunito', sans-serif;
            background: linear-gradient(160deg, #f0ebff 0%, #fdf4ff 40%, #ebf4ff 100%);
            min-height: 100vh;
            color: #2d2250;
          }

          /* ── Top Nav Bar ─────────────────────────── */
          #top-navbar {
            position: sticky;
            top: 0;
            z-index: 1000;
            background: linear-gradient(135deg, #3b1fa3 0%, #7c3aed 60%, #c026d3 100%);
            box-shadow: 0 4px 20px rgba(60,20,163,.35);
          }
          #nav-title {
            padding: .6rem 1.4rem .4rem;
            color: #fff;
            font-size: 1rem;
            font-weight: 900;
            letter-spacing: .03em;
            border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.15);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: .5rem;
          }
          #nav-title .subtitle {
            font-weight: 400;
            font-size: .8rem;
            opacity: .75;
            margin-left: .5rem;
          }
          #nav-tabs {
            display: flex;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: .5rem 1rem;
            overflow-x: auto;
            scroll-behavior: smooth;
            -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
            scrollbar-width: thin;
            scrollbar-color: rgba(255,255,255,.3) transparent;
          }
          #nav-tabs::-webkit-scrollbar { height: 4px; }
          #nav-tabs::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: rgba(255,255,255,.3); border-radius: 4px; }
          .nav-tab {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background: rgba(255,255,255,.15);
            color: #fff;
            border: 1.5px solid rgba(255,255,255,.25);
            border-radius: 50px;
            padding: .38rem 1.1rem;
            font-size: .85rem;
            font-weight: 700;
            cursor: pointer;
            transition: background .18s, transform .12s, border-color .18s;
            white-space: nowrap;
            user-select: none;
          }
          .nav-tab:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,.28); transform: translateY(-1px); }
          .nav-tab.active { background: #fff; color: #5b21b6; border-color: #fff; }
          #nav-dropdown {
            display: none;
            background: rgba(40, 10, 120, .97);
            backdrop-filter: blur(8px);
            padding: .5rem 1rem .6rem;
            border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.1);
            overflow-x: auto;
            white-space: nowrap;
            gap: 6px;
          }
          #nav-dropdown.open { display: flex; }
          .nav-sub {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            color: #e9d5ff;
            font-size: .8rem;
            font-weight: 600;
            padding: .28rem .9rem;
            border-radius: 50px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.2);
            cursor: pointer;
            transition: background .15s;
            white-space: nowrap;
          }
          .nav-sub:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,.18); color: #fff; }

          /* ── Main Content ──────────────────────────── */
          #main-content {
            max-width: 1140px;
            margin: 0 auto;
            padding: 2rem 1.4rem 4rem;
          }

          /* Hero */
          .hero {
            background: linear-gradient(135deg,#3b1fa3,#7c3aed 55%,#c026d3);
            color: #fff; border-radius: 20px; padding: 2.5rem 2rem 2rem;
            margin-bottom: 2.5rem; text-align: center; position: relative; overflow: hidden;
          }
          .hero h1 { font-size: 2.4rem; font-weight: 900; }
          .hero p  { font-size: 1.05rem; opacity: .88; margin: .5rem 0 0; }
          .hero .flags { font-size: 2.4rem; letter-spacing:.3rem; margin-bottom:.5rem; }
          .hero::before {
            content:''; position:absolute; top:-40px; right:-40px;
            width:200px; height:200px; border-radius:50%;
            background:rgba(255,255,255,.07);
          }

          /* Content Sections */
          .content-section { display: none; }
          .content-section.visible { display: block; }

          /* ── German Topic Banner ─────────────────── */
          .topic-banner {
            background: linear-gradient(135deg, #ede9fe, #fdf4ff);
            border: 2px solid #c4b5fd;
            border-radius: 16px;
            padding: 1rem 1.4rem;
            margin-bottom: 1.8rem;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 1rem;
            flex-wrap: wrap;
          }
          .topic-banner .de-word {
            font-size: 1.6rem;
            font-weight: 900;
            color: #5b21b6;
          }
          .topic-banner .de-article {
            font-size: 1rem;
            font-weight: 700;
            color: #9333ea;
            background: #e9d5ff;
            border-radius: 6px;
            padding: 2px 8px;
            margin-right: 4px;
          }
          .topic-banner .en-word {
            font-size: 1rem;
            color: #6b7280;
            font-style: italic;
          }
          .topic-banner .pron-word {
            font-size: .85rem;
            color: #9333ea;
            font-family: 'Fira Code', monospace;
          }

          /* Headings */
          h2 {
            color: #5b21b6;
            border-left: 5px solid #7c3aed;
            padding: .3rem 0 .3rem .75rem;
            margin-top: 2.4rem;
            margin-bottom: .9rem;
            font-weight: 800;
            font-size: 1.35rem;
          }
          h2:first-child { margin-top: 0; }
          h3 { color: #7c3aed; font-weight: 700; margin: 1.5rem 0 .6rem; font-size: 1.1rem; }
          h4 { color: #9333ea; font-weight: 700; margin: 1.2rem 0 .4rem; }

          /* Callouts */
          .callout-tip    { border-left: 4px solid #059669 !important; background: #ecfdf5 !important; border-radius: 10px; padding: .9rem 1rem; margin: 1rem 0; }
          .callout-note   { border-left: 4px solid #3b82f6 !important; background: #eff6ff !important; border-radius: 10px; padding: .9rem 1rem; margin: 1rem 0; }
          .callout-important { border-left: 4px solid #f59e0b !important; background: #fffbeb !important; border-radius: 10px; padding: .9rem 1rem; margin: 1rem 0; }
          .callout-warning{ border-left: 4px solid #ef4444 !important; background: #fef2f2 !important; border-radius: 10px; padding: .9rem 1rem; margin: 1rem 0; }
          .callout-note p, .callout-tip p, .callout-important p, .callout-warning p { margin: 0; font-size: .92rem; }

          /* Tables */
          table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin: 1rem 0; border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; font-size: .92rem; }
          thead tr { background: linear-gradient(90deg,#5b21b6,#7c3aed); color: #fff; }
          thead th { padding: .65rem .9rem; font-weight: 700; text-align: left; }
          tbody tr:nth-child(even) { background: #f5f0ff; }
          tbody tr:nth-child(odd)  { background: #fff; }
          tbody td { padding: .5rem .9rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #e9d5ff; }
          tbody tr:hover { background: #ede9fe; transition: background .15s; }

          /* Vocab cards */
          .vocab-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(155px,1fr)); gap: 12px; margin: 1.2rem 0; }
          .vocab-card {
            background: #fff; border-radius: 12px; padding: .9rem .7rem;
            text-align: center; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(124,58,237,.1);
            border: 1.5px solid #e9d5ff; transition: transform .15s, box-shadow .15s;
          }
          .vocab-card:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 6px 18px rgba(124,58,237,.2); }
          .vocab-card .de   { font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 800; color: #5b21b6; }
          .vocab-card .pron { font-size: .73rem; color: #9333ea; font-family: 'Fira Code', monospace; }
          .vocab-card .en   { font-size: .83rem; color: #6b7280; margin-top: 4px; }

          /* Color swatches */
          .color-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(110px,1fr)); gap: 10px; margin: 1rem 0; }
          .color-swatch { border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.12); text-align: center; font-weight: 700; }
          .color-swatch .swatch-block { height: 60px; }
          .color-swatch .swatch-label { padding: 6px 4px; font-size: .82rem; background: #fff; color: #2d2250; border-top: 1px solid #e9d5ff; }

          /* Dialogue */
          .dialogue {
            background: linear-gradient(135deg,#ede9fe,#fdf4ff);
            border-radius: 14px; padding: 1.2rem 1.4rem;
            margin: 1rem 0; border: 1.5px solid #c4b5fd;
          }
          .dialogue p { margin: .4rem 0; font-size: .93rem; }
          .dialogue .speaker-a { color: #5b21b6; font-weight: 700; }
          .dialogue .speaker-b { color: #0369a1; font-weight: 700; }

          /* Conjugation box */
          .conj-box { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 16px; margin: 1rem 0; }
          .conj-card { background: #fff; border-radius: 14px; padding: 1rem 1.2rem; border: 2px solid #e9d5ff; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(124,58,237,.08); }
          .conj-card h5 { margin: 0 0 .6rem; color: #7c3aed; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 800; }
          .conj-card table { margin: 0; font-size: .87rem; }
          .conj-card thead tr { background: #7c3aed; }

          /* Number grid */
          .num-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(100px,1fr)); gap: 8px; margin: 1rem 0; }
          .num-card { background: #fff; border-radius: 10px; padding: .6rem .4rem; text-align: center; border: 1.5px solid #e9d5ff; box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(124,58,237,.1); transition: transform .12s; }
          .num-card:hover { transform: scale(1.04); }
          .num-card .num  { font-size: 1.3rem; font-weight: 900; color: #5b21b6; }
          .num-card .word { font-size: .75rem; color: #7c3aed; font-weight: 600; }
          .num-card .pron { font-size: .67rem; color: #9ca3af; font-family:'Fira Code',monospace; }

          /* Alphabet — full highlight for all 26 rows */
          .alpha-table td:first-child { font-size: 1.15rem; font-weight: 900; color: #5b21b6; }
          .alpha-table td:nth-child(2),
          .alpha-table td:nth-child(4),
          .alpha-table td:nth-child(6) { font-family:'Fira Code',monospace; color:#7c3aed; font-size:.9rem; }
          .alpha-table td:nth-child(3),
          .alpha-table td:nth-child(5) { font-size: 1.15rem; font-weight: 900; color: #5b21b6; }

          /* Seasons */
          .season-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4,1fr); gap:12px; margin:1rem 0; }
          .season-card { border-radius:14px; padding:1rem; text-align:center; font-weight:700; }
          .season-card .icon { font-size:2.2rem; }
          .season-card .name { font-size:1rem; margin:.3rem 0 .1rem; }
          .season-card .eng  { font-size:.82rem; opacity:.75; }
          .season-card .months { font-size:.75rem; margin-top:.4rem; opacity:.65; }
          .winter { background:linear-gradient(135deg,#bfdbfe,#eff6ff); color:#1e40af; border:2px solid #93c5fd; }
          .spring { background:linear-gradient(135deg,#bbf7d0,#f0fdf4); color:#166534; border:2px solid #86efac; }
          .summer { background:linear-gradient(135deg,#fef08a,#fefce8); color:#713f12; border:2px solid #fde047; }
          .autumn { background:linear-gradient(135deg,#fed7aa,#fff7ed); color:#7c2d12; border:2px solid #fb923c; }

          /* Clock */
          .clock-row { display:flex; gap:12px; flex-wrap:wrap; margin:1rem 0; }
          .clock-item { background:#fff; border-radius:12px; padding:.7rem 1rem; border:2px solid #e9d5ff; min-width:155px; flex:1; }
          .clock-item .time { font-size:1.2rem; font-weight:900; color:#5b21b6; }
          .clock-item .de   { font-size:.9rem; color:#7c3aed; font-weight:700; }
          .clock-item .en   { font-size:.8rem; color:#6b7280; }

          /* Pronoun badges */
          .pronoun-grid { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:10px; margin:1rem 0; }
          .pronoun-badge { background:linear-gradient(135deg,#5b21b6,#7c3aed); color:#fff; border-radius:50px; padding:.45rem 1.1rem; font-weight:700; font-size:.9rem; display:flex; gap:.5rem; align-items:center; }
          .pronoun-badge .eng { font-weight:400; opacity:.8; font-size:.82rem; }

          /* Number Converter Widget */
          .num-converter {
            background: linear-gradient(135deg, #ede9fe, #fdf4ff);
            border: 2px solid #c4b5fd;
            border-radius: 18px;
            padding: 1.4rem 1.6rem;
            margin: 1.5rem 0;
          }
          .num-converter h4 { color: #5b21b6; font-size: 1.1rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; }
          .num-converter-row { display: flex; gap: 10px; align-items: center; flex-wrap: wrap; }
          .num-converter input {
            flex: 1;
            min-width: 120px;
            max-width: 220px;
            padding: .6rem .9rem;
            border: 2px solid #c4b5fd;
            border-radius: 10px;
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            font-family: 'Nunito', sans-serif;
            font-weight: 700;
            color: #3b1fa3;
            outline: none;
            transition: border-color .2s;
          }
          .num-converter input:focus { border-color: #7c3aed; }
          .num-converter button {
            background: linear-gradient(135deg,#5b21b6,#7c3aed);
            color: #fff;
            border: none;
            border-radius: 10px;
            padding: .6rem 1.4rem;
            font-size: 1rem;
            font-weight: 800;
            font-family: 'Nunito', sans-serif;
            cursor: pointer;
            transition: opacity .15s, transform .12s;
          }
          .num-converter button:hover { opacity: .88; transform: translateY(-1px); }
          .num-converter-result {
            margin-top: 1rem;
            background: #fff;
            border-radius: 12px;
            padding: 1rem 1.2rem;
            border: 2px solid #e9d5ff;
            display: none;
          }
          .num-converter-result .result-number { font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 900; color: #5b21b6; }
          .num-converter-result .result-word { font-size: 1.1rem; color: #7c3aed; font-weight: 700; margin-top: .3rem; }
          .num-converter-result .result-pron { font-size: .82rem; color: #9ca3af; font-family: 'Fira Code', monospace; margin-top: .2rem; }
          .num-converter-error { color: #ef4444; font-size: .9rem; margin-top: .6rem; display: none; }

          /* hr */
          hr { border: none; border-top: 2px solid #e9d5ff; margin: 2rem 0; }

          /* Responsive */
          @media(max-width:640px){
            .season-grid { grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; }
            .conj-box     { grid-template-columns:1fr; }
            .hero h1 { font-size: 1.7rem; }
          }
        </style>
---

```{=html}
<div id="top-navbar">
  <div id="nav-title">
    🇩🇪 German A1 Language
    <span class="subtitle">Beginner Study Notes · Click a topic to navigate</span>
  </div>
  <div id="nav-tabs">
    <div class="nav-tab active" data-section="communication" onclick="activateTab(this)">🗣️ Communication</div>
    <div class="nav-tab" data-section="countries" onclick="activateTab(this)">🌍 Countries &amp; Languages</div>
    <div class="nav-tab" data-section="personal" onclick="activateTab(this)">👤 Personal Info</div>
    <div class="nav-tab" data-section="numbers" onclick="activateTab(this)">🔢 Numbers</div>
    <div class="nav-tab" data-section="alphabet" onclick="activateTab(this)">🔤 Alphabet</div>
    <div class="nav-tab" data-section="colors" onclick="activateTab(this)">🎨 Colors</div>
    <div class="nav-tab" data-section="grammar" onclick="activateTab(this)">📐 Grammar</div>
    <div class="nav-tab" data-section="family" onclick="activateTab(this)">👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family</div>
    <div class="nav-tab" data-section="school" onclick="activateTab(this)">🏫 School</div>
    <div class="nav-tab" data-section="time" onclick="activateTab(this)">📅 Time &amp; Calendar</div>
  </div>
  <div id="nav-dropdown"></div>
</div>

<script>
const subheadings = {
  communication: [
    { label: "Introductions", id: "sub-introductions" },
    { label: "Greetings & Farewells", id: "sub-greetings" },
    { label: "Feelings & Wellbeing", id: "sub-feelings" },
  ],
  countries: [
    { label: "Countries (Länder)", id: "sub-countries-list" },
    { label: "Languages (Sprachen)", id: "sub-languages" },
  ],
  personal: [
    { label: "Names & Forms of Address", id: "sub-names" },
    { label: "Age, Address & Contact", id: "sub-contact" },
    { label: "Marital Status & Children", id: "sub-marital" },
  ],
  numbers: [
    { label: "0 – 19", id: "sub-num0" },
    { label: "20 – 99", id: "sub-num20" },
    { label: "Hundreds (100–900)", id: "sub-hundreds" },
    { label: "Thousands (1,000–10,000)", id: "sub-thousands" },
    { label: "Number Converter", id: "sub-converter" },
  ],
  alphabet: [
    { label: "The German Alphabet", id: "sub-alpha" },
    { label: "Special Characters (Umlauts)", id: "sub-umlauts" },
  ],
  colors: [
    { label: "Color Swatches", id: "sub-colorswatches" },
    { label: "Talking About Colors", id: "sub-colortalk" },
  ],
  grammar: [
    { label: "sein & haben", id: "sub-seinhabn" },
    { label: "Regular Verb Conjugation", id: "sub-regverbs" },
    { label: "Articles & Nouns", id: "sub-articles" },
    { label: "Plural Forms", id: "sub-plurals" },
    { label: "Possessive Pronouns", id: "sub-poss" },
    { label: "Personal Pronouns", id: "sub-persp" },
  ],
  family: [
    { label: "Family Members", id: "sub-familymembers" },
    { label: "Possessive Sentences", id: "sub-familysent" },
  ],
  school: [
    { label: "School Vocabulary", id: "sub-schoolvocab" },
    { label: "School Subjects", id: "sub-subjects" },
    { label: "Timetable Phrases", id: "sub-timetable" },
    { label: "Classroom Objects", id: "sub-classobj" },
    { label: "Classroom Language", id: "sub-classlang" },
  ],
  time: [
    { label: "Days of the Week", id: "sub-days" },
    { label: "Months", id: "sub-months" },
    { label: "Seasons", id: "sub-seasons" },
    { label: "Time Expressions", id: "sub-timeexpr" },
    { label: "Telling the Time", id: "sub-telling" },
  ],
};

function activateTab(el) {
  document.querySelectorAll('.nav-tab').forEach(t => t.classList.remove('active'));
  el.classList.add('active');
  const sec = el.dataset.section;
  document.querySelectorAll('.content-section').forEach(s => s.classList.remove('visible'));
  const target = document.getElementById('sec-' + sec);
  if (target) target.classList.add('visible');
  const dropdown = document.getElementById('nav-dropdown');
  const subs = subheadings[sec] || [];
  if (subs.length) {
    dropdown.innerHTML = subs.map(s =>
      `<div class="nav-sub" onclick="scrollToSub('${s.id}')">${s.label}</div>`
    ).join('');
    dropdown.classList.add('open');
  } else {
    dropdown.innerHTML = '';
    dropdown.classList.remove('open');
  }
  window.scrollTo({ top: document.getElementById('top-navbar').offsetHeight - 2, behavior: 'smooth' });
}

function scrollToSub(id) {
  const el = document.getElementById(id);
  if (!el) return;
  const offset = document.getElementById('top-navbar').offsetHeight + 12;
  const top = el.getBoundingClientRect().top + window.scrollY - offset;
  window.scrollTo({ top, behavior: 'smooth' });
}

/* ── German Number Converter (0–100,000) ── */
const ones = ['','ein','zwei','drei','vier','fünf','sechs','sieben','acht','neun',
               'zehn','elf','zwölf','dreizehn','vierzehn','fünfzehn','sechzehn',
               'siebzehn','achtzehn','neunzehn'];
const tens = ['','','zwanzig','dreißig','vierzig','fünfzig','sechzig','siebzig','achtzig','neunzig'];

function numToGerman(n) {
  if (n === 0) return 'null';
  if (n === 1) return 'eins';
  let result = '';
  if (n >= 100000) return 'hunderttausend';
  if (n >= 1000) {
    const t = Math.floor(n / 1000);
    result += (t === 1 ? 'ein' : numToGerman(t)) + 'tausend';
    n %= 1000;
    if (n === 0) return result;
  }
  if (n >= 100) {
    const h = Math.floor(n / 100);
    result += (h === 1 ? 'ein' : ones[h]) + 'hundert';
    n %= 100;
    if (n === 0) return result;
  }
  if (n >= 20) {
    const u = n % 10;
    const t = Math.floor(n / 10);
    if (u > 0) result += ones[u] + 'und' + tens[t];
    else result += tens[t];
  } else if (n > 0) {
    result += ones[n];
  }
  return result;
}

/* pronunciation approximation for display */
const pronMap = {
  'null':'[nool]','eins':'[ines]','zwei':'[tsvye]','drei':'[drye]','vier':'[feer]',
  'fünf':'[fuenf]','sechs':'[zeks]','sieben':'[ZEE-ben]','acht':'[akht]','neun':'[noyn]',
  'zehn':'[tsayn]','elf':'[elf]','zwölf':'[tsvurlf]','zwanzig':'[TSVAN-tsish]',
  'dreißig':'[DRY-ssish]','vierzig':'[FEER-tsish]','fünfzig':'[FUENF-tsish]',
  'sechzig':'[ZEKH-tsish]','siebzig':'[ZEEP-tsish]','achtzig':'[AHKH-tsish]',
  'neunzig':'[NOYN-tsish]','hundert':'[HOON-dert]','tausend':'[TOW-zent]',
};

function convertNumber() {
  const input = document.getElementById('num-input');
  const resultDiv = document.getElementById('num-result');
  const errorDiv = document.getElementById('num-error');
  const val = parseInt(input.value, 10);
  resultDiv.style.display = 'none';
  errorDiv.style.display = 'none';
  if (isNaN(val) || val < 0 || val > 100000) {
    errorDiv.textContent = '⚠️ Please enter a number between 0 and 100,000.';
    errorDiv.style.display = 'block';
    return;
  }
  const word = numToGerman(val);
  document.getElementById('res-number').textContent = val.toLocaleString('de-DE');
  document.getElementById('res-word').textContent = word;
  /* find a rough pron hint */
  let pron = '';
  for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(pronMap)) {
    if (word === k) { pron = v; break; }
  }
  document.getElementById('res-pron').textContent = pron;
  resultDiv.style.display = 'block';
}

document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
  const firstTab = document.querySelector('.nav-tab[data-section="communication"]');
  if (firstTab) activateTab(firstTab);
  const inp = document.getElementById('num-input');
  if (inp) inp.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === 'Enter') convertNumber(); });
});
</script>

<div id="main-content">
  <div class="hero">
    <div class="flags">🇩🇪 🇦🇹 🇨🇭</div>
    <h1>🇩🇪 German A1</h1>
    <p>Complete Study Notes · Beginner Level · With Pronunciation &amp; English Translations</p>
  </div>
```

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: Communication ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-communication .content-section .visible}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">die</span><span class="de-word">Kommunikation</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[ko-moo-ni-ka-TSYOHN]</div>
  <div class="en-word">Communication</div>
</div>
```

## Introductions {#sub-introductions}

> **💡 DID YOU KNOW?** German is spoken in three countries: **Deutschland** (Germany), **Österreich** (Austria), and **die Schweiz** (Switzerland). Together called the **DACH** countries.

### Formal (Sie) vs. Informal (du)

::: {.callout-note}
**Sie** (capital S) = formal/polite, used with strangers, elders, professionals.  
**du** = informal/casual, used with friends, family, children.
:::

| Context | Question | Pronunciation | Meaning |
|---------|----------|---------------|---------|
| Formal (Sie) | Wie heißen Sie? | [vee HY-sen zee] | What is your name? |
| Informal (du) | Wie heißt du? | [vee HYst doo] | What is your name? |
| Answer 1 | Ich heiße ___. | [ikh HY-seh] | My name is ___. |
| Answer 2 | Ich bin ___. | [ikh bin] | I am ___. |
| Answer 3 | Mein Name ist ___. | [mine NAH-meh ist] | My name is ___. |

### Asking Where Someone Is From

| Type | German | Pronunciation | English |
|------|--------|---------------|---------|
| Formal Q | Woher kommen Sie? | [vo-HAIR KOM-en zee] | Where do you come from? |
| Informal Q | Woher kommst du? | [vo-HAIR komst doo] | Where do you come from? |
| Answer | Ich komme aus ___. | [ikh KOM-eh owss] | I come from ___. |

### Asking Where Someone Lives

| Type | German | Pronunciation | English |
|------|--------|---------------|---------|
| Formal Q | Wo wohnen Sie? | [vo VO-nen zee] | Where do you live? |
| Informal Q | Wo wohnst du? | [vo vohnst doo] | Where do you live? |
| Answer | Ich wohne in ___. | [ikh VO-neh in] | I live in ___. |

### Self-Introduction Example

::: {.dialogue}
<p><span class="speaker-a">Ich heiße Nimesh.</span> — My name is Nimesh.</p>
<p><span class="speaker-a">Ich komme aus Sri Lanka.</span> — I come from Sri Lanka.</p>
<p><span class="speaker-a">Ich wohne in Münster.</span> — I live in Münster.</p>
:::

---

## Greetings & Farewells {#sub-greetings}

### Greetings by Time of Day

```{=html}
<div class="vocab-grid">
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Hallo!</div><div class="pron">[HA-lo]</div><div class="en">Hello – anytime</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Guten Morgen</div><div class="pron">[GOO-ten MOR-gen]</div><div class="en">Good morning</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Guten Tag</div><div class="pron">[GOO-ten tahk]</div><div class="en">Good day – daytime</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Guten Abend</div><div class="pron">[GOO-ten AH-bent]</div><div class="en">Good evening (after 6pm)</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Gute Nacht</div><div class="pron">[GOO-teh nakht]</div><div class="en">Good night – only at bedtime</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Servus!</div><div class="pron">[ZER-voos]</div><div class="en">Hi/Bye – Austrian/Bavarian</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Grüß Gott!</div><div class="pron">[groos got]</div><div class="en">Hello – Southern Germany</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Moin!</div><div class="pron">[moyn]</div><div class="en">Hello – Northern Germany</div></div>
</div>
```

### Farewells

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| Tschüs / Tschau / Ciao! | [choos / chow] | Bye |
| Auf Wiedersehen! | [owf VEE-der-zayn] | Goodbye (formal) |
| Bis morgen! | [biss MOR-gen] | See you tomorrow |
| Bis dann! | [biss dan] | See you then |
| Bis später! | [biss SHPAY-ter] | See you later |
| Bis gleich! | [biss glysh] | See you soon |
| Schönes Wochenende! | [SHUR-nes VO-khen-en-deh] | Have a nice weekend! |
| Mach's gut! | [makhs goot] | Take care |

### Useful Everyday Expressions

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| Danke / Vielen Dank | [DAN-keh] | Thank you / Many thanks |
| Bitte | [BIT-eh] | Please / You're welcome |
| Entschuldigung | [ent-SHOOL-dee-goong] | Excuse me |
| Es tut mir leid | [ess toot meer lyte] | I am sorry |
| Gesundheit! | [geh-ZOONT-hyte] | Bless you (after sneezing) |
| Prost! / Zum Wohl! | [prohst / tsoom vohl] | Cheers! |
| Ja / Nein | [yah / nine] | Yes / No |

---

## Feelings & Wellbeing {#sub-feelings}

### How to Ask

| German | Pronunciation | English | Register |
|--------|---------------|---------|----------|
| Wie geht es Ihnen? | [vee gayt ess EE-nen] | How are you? | Formal |
| Wie geht es dir? | [vee gayt ess deer] | How are you? | Informal |
| Wie geht's? | [vee gayts] | How's it going? | Casual |
| Alles klar? | [AL-less klahr] | Everything OK? | Informal |

### Sample Conversation

::: {.dialogue}
<p><span class="speaker-a">A: Wie geht es Ihnen?</span> — How are you?</p>
<p><span class="speaker-b">B: Es geht mir gut, danke. Und Ihnen?</span> — I'm fine, thank you. And you?</p>
<p><span class="speaker-a">A: Es geht mir auch gut, danke.</span> — I'm also fine, thank you.</p>
:::

### Responses — from 😊 to 😞

```{=html}
<div class="vocab-grid">
  <div class="vocab-card" style="border-color:#10b981"><div class="de">Fantastisch!</div><div class="en">Fantastic!</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card" style="border-color:#10b981"><div class="de">Sehr gut!</div><div class="en">Very good!</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card" style="border-color:#10b981"><div class="de">Super / Prima</div><div class="en">Great!</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card" style="border-color:#3b82f6"><div class="de">Gut</div><div class="en">Good</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card" style="border-color:#3b82f6"><div class="de">Ganz gut</div><div class="en">Pretty good</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card" style="border-color:#f59e0b"><div class="de">Es geht</div><div class="en">Okay / So-so</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card" style="border-color:#f59e0b"><div class="de">So la la</div><div class="en">So-so</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card" style="border-color:#ef4444"><div class="de">Nicht gut</div><div class="en">Not good</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card" style="border-color:#ef4444"><div class="de">Sehr schlecht</div><div class="en">Very bad</div></div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-tip}
**'auch'** = also/too.  **'Und dir?'** (informal) / **'Und Ihnen?'** (formal) = And you?
:::

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: Countries ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-countries .content-section}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">die</span><span class="de-word">Länder und Sprachen</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[LEND-er oont SHPRAH-khen]</div>
  <div class="en-word">Countries and Languages</div>
</div>
```

## Countries (Länder) {#sub-countries-list}

::: {.callout-note}
Most countries have **NO article**. Exceptions: **die Schweiz**, **die Türkei**, **die Ukraine**, **die USA** (plural → den USA).
:::

| German | Pronunciation | English | Ich komme aus... |
|--------|---------------|---------|-----------------|
| Deutschland | [DOYCH-lant] | Germany | ... Deutschland |
| Österreich | [OH-ster-rike] | Austria | ... Österreich |
| die Schweiz | [dee shvites] | Switzerland | ... **der** Schweiz |
| die Türkei | [dee tuer-KY] | Turkey | ... **der** Türkei |
| die Ukraine | [dee oo-KRAY-neh] | Ukraine | ... **der** Ukraine |
| die USA | [dee oo-es-AH] | USA | ... **den** USA |
| Sri Lanka | [shree LAN-kah] | Sri Lanka | ... Sri Lanka |
| England | [ENG-lant] | England | ... England |

---

## Languages (Sprachen) {#sub-languages}

```{=html}
<div class="vocab-grid">
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Deutsch</div><div class="pron">[doych]</div><div class="en">German</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Englisch</div><div class="pron">[ENG-lish]</div><div class="en">English</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Französisch</div><div class="pron">[fran-TSUR-zish]</div><div class="en">French</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Spanisch</div><div class="pron">[SHPAH-nish]</div><div class="en">Spanish</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Singhalesisch</div><div class="pron">[zing-hah-LAY-zish]</div><div class="en">Sinhala</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Tamil</div><div class="pron">[TAH-mil]</div><div class="en">Tamil</div></div>
</div>
```

### Asking About Languages

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| Welche Sprachen sprechen Sie? | [VEL-kheh SHPRAH-khen SHPREKH-en zee] | Which languages do you speak? (formal) |
| Welche Sprachen sprichst du? | [VEL-kheh SHPRAH-khen shprikhst doo] | Which languages do you speak? (informal) |
| Ich spreche Deutsch und etwas Englisch. | [ikh SHPREKH-eh...ET-vass] | I speak German and a little English. |
| Was ist Ihre Muttersprache? | [vass ist EE-reh MOO-ter-shprah-kheh] | What is your mother tongue? |

::: {.callout-tip}
**'etwas'** or **'ein bisschen'** = a little.  
*'Ich spreche ein bisschen Deutsch.'* — I speak a little German.
:::

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: Personal Info ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-personal .content-section}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">die</span><span class="de-word">Persönliche Informationen</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[per-ZURN-likh-eh in-for-ma-TSYOH-nen]</div>
  <div class="en-word">Personal Information</div>
</div>
```

## Names & Forms of Address {#sub-names}

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| der Vorname | [dare FOR-nah-meh] | First / Given name |
| der Nachname / Familienname | [NAHKH-nah-meh] | Surname / Last name |
| Frau | [frow] | Ms. / Mrs. |
| Herr | [hair] | Mr. |
| Wie ist Ihr Vorname? | [vee ist eer FOR-nah-meh] | What is your first name? (formal) |
| Mein Vorname ist ___. | [mine FOR-nah-meh ist] | My first name is ___. |

---

## Age, Address & Contact {#sub-contact}

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| Wie alt sind Sie? | [vee alt zint zee] | How old are you? (formal) |
| Wie alt bist du? | [vee alt bist doo] | How old are you? (informal) |
| Ich bin 29 Jahre alt. | [ikh bin...YAH-reh alt] | I am 29 years old. |
| die Straße | [dee SHTRAH-seh] | Street |
| die Hausnummer | [dee HOWS-noo-mer] | House number |
| die Postleitzahl (PLZ) | [dee POST-lite-tsahl] | Postal code |
| die Telefonnummer | [dee teh-leh-FON-noo-mer] | Phone number |
| die Handynummer | [dee HEN-dee-noo-mer] | Mobile number |
| die E-Mail-Adresse | [dee ee-MAIL ah-DRESS-eh] | Email address |
| @ = at | [ET] | at sign |
| . = Punkt | [poonkt] | dot |

---

## Marital Status & Children {#sub-marital}

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| Sind Sie verheiratet? | [zint zee fer-HY-rah-tet] | Are you married? (formal) |
| Ja, ich bin verheiratet. | [yah, ikh bin] | Yes, I am married. |
| Nein, ich bin ledig. | [nine, ikh bin LAY-dikh] | No, I am single. |
| geschieden | [geh-SHEE-den] | divorced |
| verwitwet | [fer-VIT-vet] | widowed |
| Haben Sie Kinder? | [HAH-ben zee KIN-der] | Do you have children? (formal) |
| Nein, ich habe keine Kinder. | [nine, ikh HAH-beh KY-neh] | No, I have no children. |
| Ja, ich habe zwei Kinder. | [yah, ikh HAH-beh tsvye] | Yes, I have two children. |

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: Numbers ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-numbers .content-section}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">die</span><span class="de-word">Zahlen</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[TSAH-len]</div>
  <div class="en-word">Numbers</div>
</div>
```

## 0 – 19 {#sub-num0}

```{=html}
<div class="num-grid">
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">0</div><div class="word">null</div><div class="pron">[nool]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">1</div><div class="word">eins</div><div class="pron">[ines]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">2</div><div class="word">zwei</div><div class="pron">[tsvye]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">3</div><div class="word">drei</div><div class="pron">[drye]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">4</div><div class="word">vier</div><div class="pron">[feer]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">5</div><div class="word">fünf</div><div class="pron">[fuenf]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">6</div><div class="word">sechs</div><div class="pron">[zeks]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">7</div><div class="word">sieben</div><div class="pron">[ZEE-ben]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">8</div><div class="word">acht</div><div class="pron">[akht]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">9</div><div class="word">neun</div><div class="pron">[noyn]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">10</div><div class="word">zehn</div><div class="pron">[tsayn]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">11</div><div class="word">elf</div><div class="pron">[elf]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">12</div><div class="word">zwölf</div><div class="pron">[tsvurlf]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">13</div><div class="word">dreizehn</div><div class="pron">[DRYE-tsayn]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">14</div><div class="word">vierzehn</div><div class="pron">[FEER-tsayn]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">15</div><div class="word">fünfzehn</div><div class="pron">[FUENF-tsayn]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">16</div><div class="word">sechzehn</div><div class="pron">[ZEKH-tsayn]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">17</div><div class="word">siebzehn</div><div class="pron">[ZEEP-tsayn]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">18</div><div class="word">achtzehn</div><div class="pron">[AHKHT-tsayn]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">19</div><div class="word">neunzehn</div><div class="pron">[NOYN-tsayn]</div></div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-warning}
⚠️ **16 = sechzehn** (NOT sechszehn — the 's' drops!) · **17 = siebzehn** (NOT siebenzehn — the 'en' drops!)
:::

---

## 20 – 99 {#sub-num20}

::: {.callout-note}
German builds compound numbers by saying the **unit FIRST**, then **'und'**, then the ten.  
**21 = einundzwanzig** (one-and-twenty) — opposite to English!  
The **-zig** suffix sounds like **[-tsish]**.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="num-grid">
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">20</div><div class="word">zwanzig</div><div class="pron">[TSVAN-tsish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">30</div><div class="word">dreißig</div><div class="pron">[DRY-ssish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">40</div><div class="word">vierzig</div><div class="pron">[FEER-tsish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">50</div><div class="word">fünfzig</div><div class="pron">[FUENF-tsish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">60</div><div class="word">sechzig</div><div class="pron">[ZEKH-tsish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">70</div><div class="word">siebzig</div><div class="pron">[ZEEP-tsish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">80</div><div class="word">achtzig</div><div class="pron">[AHKH-tsish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">90</div><div class="word">neunzig</div><div class="pron">[NOYN-tsish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">21</div><div class="word">einund­zwanzig</div><div class="pron">[INE-oont-TSVAN-tsish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">35</div><div class="word">fünfund­dreißig</div><div class="pron">[FUENF-oont-DRY-ssish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">47</div><div class="word">siebenund­vierzig</div><div class="pron">[ZEE-ben-oont-FEER-tsish]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">99</div><div class="word">neunund­neunzig</div><div class="pron">[NOYN-oont-NOYN-tsish]</div></div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-tip}
**Pattern for -zig numbers:** unit + **und** + ten  
e.g. 63 = **drei**und**sechzig** · 78 = **acht**und**siebzig**  
Note: **dreißig** (30) is spelled with **-ßig**, not -zig — it's the only exception!
:::

---

## Hundreds (100 – 900) {#sub-hundreds}

::: {.callout-note}
Hundreds are formed by placing the digit before **hundert**.  
100 can be **einhundert** or just **hundert**. No space is written between parts.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="num-grid">
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">100</div><div class="word">hundert</div><div class="pron">[HOON-dert]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">200</div><div class="word">zwei­hundert</div><div class="pron">[TSVYE-hoon-dert]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">300</div><div class="word">drei­hundert</div><div class="pron">[DRYE-hoon-dert]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">400</div><div class="word">vier­hundert</div><div class="pron">[FEER-hoon-dert]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">500</div><div class="word">fünf­hundert</div><div class="pron">[FUENF-hoon-dert]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">600</div><div class="word">sechs­hundert</div><div class="pron">[ZEKS-hoon-dert]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">700</div><div class="word">sieben­hundert</div><div class="pron">[ZEE-ben-hoon-dert]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">800</div><div class="word">acht­hundert</div><div class="pron">[AHKHT-hoon-dert]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">900</div><div class="word">neun­hundert</div><div class="pron">[NOYN-hoon-dert]</div></div>
</div>
```

### Combined Hundreds Examples

| # | German | Pattern |
|---|--------|---------|
| 101 | einhunderteins | hundert + eins |
| 115 | einhundertfünfzehn | hundert + fünfzehn |
| 250 | zweihundertfünfzig | zweihundert + fünfzig |
| 316 | dreihundertsechzehn | dreihundert + sechzehn |
| 472 | vierhundertzweiundsiebzig | vierhundert + zweiundsiebzig |
| 999 | neunhundertneunundneunzig | neunhundert + neunundneunzig |

---

## Thousands (1,000 – 10,000) {#sub-thousands}

::: {.callout-note}
Thousands are formed by placing the digit before **tausend**.  
1,000 = **eintausend** or just **tausend**. No space within a compound number.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="num-grid">
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">1.000</div><div class="word">tausend</div><div class="pron">[TOW-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">2.000</div><div class="word">zwei­tausend</div><div class="pron">[TSVYE-tow-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">3.000</div><div class="word">drei­tausend</div><div class="pron">[DRYE-tow-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">4.000</div><div class="word">vier­tausend</div><div class="pron">[FEER-tow-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">5.000</div><div class="word">fünf­tausend</div><div class="pron">[FUENF-tow-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">6.000</div><div class="word">sechs­tausend</div><div class="pron">[ZEKS-tow-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">7.000</div><div class="word">sieben­tausend</div><div class="pron">[ZEE-ben-tow-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">8.000</div><div class="word">acht­tausend</div><div class="pron">[AHKHT-tow-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">9.000</div><div class="word">neun­tausend</div><div class="pron">[NOYN-tow-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card"><div class="num">10.000</div><div class="word">zehn­tausend</div><div class="pron">[TSAYN-tow-zent]</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#c4b5fd"><div class="num" style="font-size:1rem">100.000</div><div class="word">hundert­tausend</div><div class="pron">[HOON-dert-tow-zent]</div></div>
</div>
```

### Combined Thousands Examples

| # | German |
|---|--------|
| 1,001 | eintausendeins |
| 2,500 | zweitausendfünfhundert |
| 5,732 | fünftausendsiebenhundertzweiunddreißig |
| 12,000 | zwölftausend |
| 25,000 | fünfundzwanzigtausend |
| 75,361 | fünfundsiebzigtausend­dreihundert­einundsechzig |
| 100,000 | hunderttausend |

::: {.callout-tip}
🇩🇪 **German uses a period as a thousands separator** and a comma as the decimal separator — the opposite of English!  
1.000 = one thousand · 1,5 = one and a half
:::

---

## Number Converter {#sub-converter}

```{=html}
<div class="num-converter">
  <h4>🔢 Type a number (0 – 100,000) → get the German word</h4>
  <div class="num-converter-row">
    <input type="number" id="num-input" min="0" max="100000" placeholder="e.g. 4567" />
    <button onclick="convertNumber()">Übersetzen →</button>
  </div>
  <div class="num-converter-error" id="num-error"></div>
  <div class="num-converter-result" id="num-result">
    <div class="result-number" id="res-number"></div>
    <div class="result-word" id="res-word"></div>
    <div class="result-pron" id="res-pron"></div>
  </div>
</div>
```

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: Alphabet ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-alphabet .content-section}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">das</span><span class="de-word">Alphabet</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[al-fah-BAYT]</div>
  <div class="en-word">The Alphabet</div>
</div>
```

## The German Alphabet {#sub-alpha}

::: {.callout-note}
German has **26 standard letters** plus **4 special characters**: Ä/ä, Ö/ö, Ü/ü (Umlauts) and **ß** (Eszett).  
**ALL nouns are capitalized** in German, regardless of position in the sentence!
:::

```{=html}
<table class="alpha-table">
<thead><tr><th>Letter</th><th>German Name</th><th>Letter</th><th>German Name</th><th>Letter</th><th>German Name</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>A a</td><td>ah</td>        <td>J j</td><td>yot</td>      <td>S s</td><td>ess</td></tr>
<tr><td>B b</td><td>beh</td>       <td>K k</td><td>kah</td>      <td>T t</td><td>teh</td></tr>
<tr><td>C c</td><td>tseh</td>      <td>L l</td><td>ell</td>      <td>U u</td><td>oo</td></tr>
<tr><td>D d</td><td>deh</td>       <td>M m</td><td>emm</td>      <td>V v</td><td>fow</td></tr>
<tr><td>E e</td><td>eh</td>        <td>N n</td><td>enn</td>      <td>W w</td><td>veh</td></tr>
<tr><td>F f</td><td>eff</td>       <td>O o</td><td>oh</td>       <td>X x</td><td>iks</td></tr>
<tr><td>G g</td><td>geh</td>       <td>P p</td><td>peh</td>      <td>Y y</td><td>üp-si-lon</td></tr>
<tr><td>H h</td><td>hah</td>       <td>Q q</td><td>koo</td>      <td>Z z</td><td>tset</td></tr>
<tr><td>I i</td><td>ee</td>        <td>R r</td><td>err</td>      <td></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
```

::: {.callout-tip}
**Spelling out a name in German:** Germans use city names as reference.  
e.g. "H wie Hamburg, E wie Emil, R wie Richard" = H-E-R  
Use: A-Anton, B-Berta, C-Cäsar, D-Dora, E-Emil, F-Friedrich, G-Gustav, H-Heinrich, I-Ida, J-Julius, K-Kaufmann, L-Ludwig, M-Martha, N-Nordpol, O-Otto, P-Paula, Q-Quelle, R-Richard, S-Samuel, T-Theodor, U-Ulrich, V-Viktor, W-Wilhelm, X-Xanthippe, Y-Ypsilon, Z-Zacharias
:::

---

## Special Characters (Umlauts & ß) {#sub-umlauts}

| Character | Name | Sounds like | Examples | Keyboard alternative |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|----------------------|
| **Ä / ä** | A-Umlaut | 'e' in 'bed' | Äpfel (apples), Käse (cheese) | ä → **ae** |
| **Ö / ö** | O-Umlaut | 'u' in 'burn' | Möhren (carrots), Österreich | ö → **oe** |
| **Ü / ü** | U-Umlaut | French 'u' | Tür (door), müde (tired) | ü → **ue** |
| **ß** | Eszett / scharfes S | double 's' | heißen, Straße, groß | ß → **ss** |

::: {.callout-tip}
No umlaut on your keyboard? Write: **ä = ae**, **ö = oe**, **ü = ue**, **ß = ss**.  
Example: Müller = **Mueller**, Straße = **Strasse**.
:::

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: Colors ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-colors .content-section}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">die</span><span class="de-word">Farben</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[FAR-ben]</div>
  <div class="en-word">Colors / Colours</div>
</div>
```

## Color Swatches {#sub-colorswatches}

```{=html}
<div class="color-grid">
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#e53e3e"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>rot</b> · red</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#3182ce"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>blau</b> · blue</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#38a169"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>grün</b> · green</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#d69e2e"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>gelb</b> · yellow</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#1a202c"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>schwarz</b> · black</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#f7fafc;border:1px solid #e2e8f0"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>weiß</b> · white</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#718096"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>grau</b> · grey</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#92400e"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>braun</b> · brown</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#ed8936"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>orange</b> · orange</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#ed64a6"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>rosa/pink</b> · pink</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#805ad5"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>lila</b> · purple</div></div>
  <div class="color-swatch"><div class="swatch-block" style="background:#0bc5ea"></div><div class="swatch-label"><b>türkis</b> · turquoise</div></div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Add **hell-** (light) or **dunkel-** (dark) as a prefix:  
**hellgrün** = light green · **dunkelblau** = dark blue
:::

---

## Talking About Colors {#sub-colortalk}

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| Was ist deine Lieblingsfarbe? | [vass ist DY-neh LEEB-lings-far-beh] | What is your favourite color? |
| Meine Lieblingsfarbe ist ___. | [MY-neh LEEB-lings-far-beh ist] | My favourite color is ___. |
| Magst du blau? | [mahkst doo blow] | Do you like blue? |
| Ja, blau mag ich. | [yah blow mahk ikh] | Yes, I like blue. |
| Ja, mir gefällt rot. | [yah meer geh-FELT roht] | Yes, I like red. |

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: Grammar ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-grammar .content-section}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">die</span><span class="de-word">Grammatik</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[gra-MAH-tik]</div>
  <div class="en-word">Grammar</div>
</div>
```

## Verbs: sein & haben {#sub-seinhabn}

::: {.callout-important}
**'sein'** (to be) and **'haben'** (to have) are the **TWO most important verbs** in German. They are irregular — memorize them!
:::

```{=html}
<div class="conj-box">
  <div class="conj-card">
    <h5>🔵 sein — to be (Present)</h5>
    <table>
      <thead><tr><th>Pronoun</th><th>Form</th><th>Pron.</th></tr></thead>
      <tbody>
        <tr><td>ich</td><td><b>bin</b></td><td>[bin]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>du</td><td><b>bist</b></td><td>[bist]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>er/sie/es</td><td><b>ist</b></td><td>[ist]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>wir</td><td><b>sind</b></td><td>[zint]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>ihr</td><td><b>seid</b></td><td>[zite]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Sie/sie</td><td><b>sind</b></td><td>[zint]</td></tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
  </div>
  <div class="conj-card">
    <h5>🟢 haben — to have (Present)</h5>
    <table>
      <thead><tr><th>Pronoun</th><th>Form</th><th>Pron.</th></tr></thead>
      <tbody>
        <tr><td>ich</td><td><b>habe</b></td><td>[HAH-beh]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>du</td><td><b>hast</b></td><td>[hast]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>er/sie/es</td><td><b>hat</b></td><td>[hat]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>wir</td><td><b>haben</b></td><td>[HAH-ben]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>ihr</td><td><b>habt</b></td><td>[hahbt]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Sie/sie</td><td><b>haben</b></td><td>[HAH-ben]</td></tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
  </div>
  <div class="conj-card">
    <h5>🔵 waren — were (Past)</h5>
    <table>
      <thead><tr><th>Pronoun</th><th>Form</th></tr></thead>
      <tbody>
        <tr><td>ich</td><td><b>war</b> [var]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>du</td><td><b>warst</b> [varst]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>er/sie/es</td><td><b>war</b> [var]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>wir</td><td><b>waren</b> [VAH-ren]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>ihr</td><td><b>wart</b> [vart]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Sie/sie</td><td><b>waren</b> [VAH-ren]</td></tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
  </div>
  <div class="conj-card">
    <h5>🟢 hatten — had (Past)</h5>
    <table>
      <thead><tr><th>Pronoun</th><th>Form</th></tr></thead>
      <tbody>
        <tr><td>ich</td><td><b>hatte</b> [HAT-eh]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>du</td><td><b>hattest</b> [HAT-test]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>er/sie/es</td><td><b>hatte</b> [HAT-eh]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>wir</td><td><b>hatten</b> [HAT-en]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>ihr</td><td><b>hattet</b> [HAT-tet]</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Sie/sie</td><td><b>hatten</b> [HAT-en]</td></tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-tip}
**WORD ORDER RULE:** The verb is **ALWAYS in position 2** in a main sentence.  
✅ *'Gestern war ich...'* — NOT ❌ *'Gestern ich war...'*
:::

---

## Regular Verb Conjugation {#sub-regverbs}

::: {.callout-note}
**99% of German verbs** end in **'-en'** in their infinitive form.  
Remove **'-en'** → get the **stem** → add the correct ending.
:::

### Standard Endings

| Pronoun | Ending | kommen | wohnen | trinken |
|---------|--------|--------|--------|---------|
| ich | **-e** | komme | wohne | trinke |
| du | **-st** | kommst | wohnst | trinkst |
| er/sie/es | **-t** | kommt | wohnt | trinkt |
| wir | **-en** | kommen | wohnen | trinken |
| ihr | **-t** | kommt | wohnt | trinkt |
| Sie/sie | **-en** | kommen | wohnen | trinken |

### Verbs Ending in -t / -d (insert extra 'e')

::: {.callout-note}
If the verb stem ends in **'t'** or **'d'**, insert an extra **'e'** before -st and -t.
:::

| Pronoun | arbeiten | warten | reden |
|---------|----------|--------|-------|
| ich | arbeite | warte | rede |
| du | arbeit**e**st | wart**e**st | red**e**st |
| er/sie/es | arbeit**e**t | wart**e**t | red**e**t |
| wir | arbeiten | warten | reden |
| ihr | arbeitet | wartet | redet |
| Sie/sie | arbeiten | warten | reden |

### Irregular Verbs (stem-vowel change)

| Pronoun | essen (eat) | lesen (read) | heißen (be named) |
|---------|-------------|--------------|-------------------|
| ich | esse | lese | heiße |
| du | **isst** | **liest** | heißt |
| er/sie/es | **isst** | **liest** | heißt |
| wir | essen | lesen | heißen |
| ihr | esst | lest | heißt |
| Sie/sie | essen | lesen | heißen |

### Common Verb Vocabulary

```{=html}
<div class="vocab-grid">
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">kommen</div><div class="pron">[KOM-en]</div><div class="en">to come</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">wohnen</div><div class="pron">[VO-nen]</div><div class="en">to live / reside</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">gehen</div><div class="pron">[GAY-en]</div><div class="en">to go</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">trinken</div><div class="pron">[TRIN-ken]</div><div class="en">to drink</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">essen</div><div class="pron">[ES-sen]</div><div class="en">to eat</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">kochen</div><div class="pron">[KO-khen]</div><div class="en">to cook</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">schlafen</div><div class="pron">[SHLAH-fen]</div><div class="en">to sleep</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">sprechen</div><div class="pron">[SHPREKH-en]</div><div class="en">to speak</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">arbeiten</div><div class="pron">[AR-by-ten]</div><div class="en">to work</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">lesen</div><div class="pron">[LAY-zen]</div><div class="en">to read</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">schreiben</div><div class="pron">[SHRY-ben]</div><div class="en">to write</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">lernen</div><div class="pron">[LAIR-nen]</div><div class="en">to learn</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">fahren</div><div class="pron">[FAH-ren]</div><div class="en">to drive</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">fliegen</div><div class="pron">[FLEE-gen]</div><div class="en">to fly</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">spielen</div><div class="pron">[SHPEE-len]</div><div class="en">to play</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">schwimmen</div><div class="pron">[SHVIM-en]</div><div class="en">to swim</div></div>
</div>
```

---

## Articles & Nouns {#sub-articles}

::: {.callout-important}
German nouns have **THREE genders**: **Maskulin** (der), **Feminin** (die), **Neutrum** (das).  
The plural article for ALL genders is **'die'**. Always learn the article with every noun!
:::

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex;gap:12px;flex-wrap:wrap;margin:1rem 0;">
  <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#dbeafe,#eff6ff);border-radius:14px;padding:1.2rem;text-align:center;border:2px solid #93c5fd;">
    <div style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:900;color:#1d4ed8">der</div>
    <div style="font-size:.9rem;color:#1e40af;font-weight:700">Maskulin (m)</div>
    <div style="font-size:.8rem;color:#3b82f6;margin-top:.4rem">der Computer<br>der Mann</div>
  </div>
  <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#fce7f3,#fdf2f8);border-radius:14px;padding:1.2rem;text-align:center;border:2px solid #f9a8d4;">
    <div style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:900;color:#be185d">die</div>
    <div style="font-size:.9rem;color:#9d174d;font-weight:700">Feminin (f)</div>
    <div style="font-size:.8rem;color:#ec4899;margin-top:.4rem">die Tasche<br>die Frau</div>
  </div>
  <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#d1fae5,#ecfdf5);border-radius:14px;padding:1.2rem;text-align:center;border:2px solid #6ee7b7;">
    <div style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:900;color:#065f46">das</div>
    <div style="font-size:.9rem;color:#047857;font-weight:700">Neutrum (n)</div>
    <div style="font-size:.8rem;color:#10b981;margin-top:.4rem">das Buch<br>das Kind</div>
  </div>
  <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#fef3c7,#fffbeb);border-radius:14px;padding:1.2rem;text-align:center;border:2px solid #fcd34d;">
    <div style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:900;color:#92400e">die</div>
    <div style="font-size:.9rem;color:#78350f;font-weight:700">Plural (pl)</div>
    <div style="font-size:.8rem;color:#d97706;margin-top:.4rem">die Bücher<br>die Kinder</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Definite / Indefinite / Negation

| | Maskulin | Feminin | Neutrum | Plural |
|--|----------|---------|---------|--------|
| **the** (definite) | **der** | **die** | **das** | **die** |
| **a/an** (indefinite) | **ein** | **eine** | **ein** | — |
| **not a / no** (negation) | **kein** | **keine** | **kein** | **keine** |

### Example Sentences

| German | English |
|--------|---------|
| Das ist **der** Kuli. / Das ist **ein** Kuli. / Das ist **kein** Kuli. | This is the pen. / a pen. / not a pen. (m) |
| Das ist **die** Tasche. / Das ist **eine** Tasche. / Das ist **keine** Tasche. | This is the bag. / a bag. / not a bag. (f) |
| Das sind **die** Bücher. / Das sind **keine** Bücher. | These are the books. / not books. (pl) |

---

## Plural Forms {#sub-plurals}

::: {.callout-note}
German plurals are complex — many patterns exist. Best approach: **learn each noun WITH its plural from the start**.
:::

| Pattern | Rule | Singular | Plural | English |
|---------|------|----------|--------|---------|
| **-e** | Add -e | der Tag | die Tage | day/days |
| **-̈e** | Umlaut + -e | der Arzt | die Ärzte | doctor/doctors |
| **-er** | Add -er | das Kind | die Kinder | child/children |
| **-̈er** | Umlaut + -er | das Buch | die Bücher | book/books |
| **-n** | Add -n | die Stunde | die Stunden | hour/hours |
| **-en** | Add -en | der Patient | die Patienten | patient/patients |
| **-s** | Add -s | das Auto | die Autos | car/cars |
| **ü--** | Umlaut only | die Mutter | die Mütter | mother/mothers |
| **—** | No change | der Kilometer | die Kilometer | kilometer/s |

---

## Possessive Pronouns {#sub-poss}

::: {.callout-note}
Possessive pronouns change their endings depending on the **gender** of the noun that follows.
:::

| Pronoun | Meaning | der (m) | die (f) | das (n) | die (pl) |
|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| ich | I / my | mein | meine | mein | meine |
| du | you / your | dein | deine | dein | deine |
| er | he / his | sein | seine | sein | seine |
| sie | she / her | ihr | ihre | ihr | ihre |
| wir | we / our | unser | unsere | unser | unsere |
| ihr | you all / your | euer | eure | euer | eure |
| **Sie** | you (formal) | **Ihr** | **Ihre** | **Ihr** | **Ihre** |
| sie | they / their | ihr | ihre | ihr | ihre |

---

## Personal Pronouns {#sub-persp}

```{=html}
<div class="pronoun-grid">
  <div class="pronoun-badge">ich <span class="eng">I</span></div>
  <div class="pronoun-badge">du <span class="eng">you (informal)</span></div>
  <div class="pronoun-badge">er <span class="eng">he</span></div>
  <div class="pronoun-badge">sie <span class="eng">she</span></div>
  <div class="pronoun-badge">es <span class="eng">it</span></div>
  <div class="pronoun-badge">wir <span class="eng">we</span></div>
  <div class="pronoun-badge">ihr <span class="eng">you all</span></div>
  <div class="pronoun-badge" style="background:linear-gradient(135deg,#059669,#10b981)">Sie <span class="eng">you (FORMAL)</span></div>
  <div class="pronoun-badge">sie <span class="eng">they</span></div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**'Sie'** (formal you, capital S) sounds exactly like **'sie'** (she/they, lowercase s).  
Context and verb conjugation tell them apart.
:::

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: Family ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-family .content-section}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">die</span><span class="de-word">Familie</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[fah-MEE-lee-eh]</div>
  <div class="en-word">Family</div>
</div>
```

## Family Members {#sub-familymembers}

```{=html}
<div style="background:linear-gradient(135deg,#fdf4ff,#ede9fe);border-radius:16px;padding:1.4rem;margin:1rem 0;border:2px solid #e9d5ff;">
  <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(220px,1fr));gap:10px;">
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #3b82f6">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#1d4ed8">👨 der Vater / Papa</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">father · die Eltern (parents)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #ec4899">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#be185d">👩 die Mutter / Mama</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">mother · die Eltern (parents)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #3b82f6">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#1d4ed8">👦 der Bruder</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">brother · die Geschwister (siblings)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #ec4899">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#be185d">👧 die Schwester</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">sister · die Geschwister (siblings)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #3b82f6">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#1d4ed8">👴 der Großvater / Opa</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">grandfather · die Großeltern</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #ec4899">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#be185d">👵 die Großmutter / Oma</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">grandmother · die Großeltern</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #3b82f6">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#1d4ed8">👦 der Sohn</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">son · die Kinder (children)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #ec4899">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#be185d">👧 die Tochter</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">daughter · die Kinder (children)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #3b82f6">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#1d4ed8">🤵 der Ehemann</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">husband · das Ehepaar (couple)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #ec4899">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#be185d">👰 die Ehefrau</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">wife · das Ehepaar (couple)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #3b82f6">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#1d4ed8">🧑 der Onkel</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">uncle · die Verwandten (relatives)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #ec4899">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#be185d">👩 die Tante</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">aunt · die Verwandten (relatives)</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #3b82f6">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#1d4ed8">🧑 der Cousin</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">male cousin</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #ec4899">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#be185d">👩 die Cousine</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">female cousin</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #3b82f6">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#1d4ed8">🧑 der Neffe</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">nephew</div>
    </div>
    <div style="background:#fff;border-radius:10px;padding:.7rem 1rem;border-left:4px solid #ec4899">
      <div style="font-weight:800;color:#be185d">👧 die Nichte</div><div style="font-size:.82rem;color:#6b7280">niece</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

## Possessive Sentences {#sub-familysent}

| German | English |
|--------|---------|
| Das ist meine Mutter. | This is my mother. |
| Das ist unser Vater. | This is our father. |
| Das ist dein Bruder. | This is your brother. |
| Das sind ihre Eltern. | These are their parents. |
| Das ist sein Onkel. | This is his uncle. |
| Das ist mein Buch. | This is my book. |
| Das ist unser Haus. | This is our house. |
| Das ist meine Brille. | This is my glasses. |
| Das ist mein Kind. | This is my child. |

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: School ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-school .content-section}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">die</span><span class="de-word">Schule</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[SHOO-leh]</div>
  <div class="en-word">School</div>
</div>
```

## School Vocabulary {#sub-schoolvocab}

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| die Schule | [dee SHOO-leh] | school |
| das Fach / die Fächer | [dass fakh / dee FEH-kher] | subject / subjects |
| der Stundenplan | [dare SHTOON-den-plan] | timetable |
| die Stunde | [dee SHTOON-deh] | period (1 hour) |
| die Pause | [dee POW-zeh] | break / interval |
| die Klasse | [dee KLAS-seh] | class |
| frei | [fry] | free (no class) |

---

## School Subjects {#sub-subjects}

```{=html}
<div class="vocab-grid">
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Mathematik</div><div class="en">Mathematics</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Deutsch</div><div class="en">German</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Englisch</div><div class="en">English</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Französisch</div><div class="en">French</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Spanisch</div><div class="en">Spanish</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Geographie</div><div class="en">Geography</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Geschichte</div><div class="en">History</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Physik</div><div class="en">Physics</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Chemie</div><div class="en">Chemistry</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Biologie</div><div class="en">Biology</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Informatik</div><div class="en">IT / Computer Science</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Kunst</div><div class="en">Art</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Musik</div><div class="en">Music</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Sport</div><div class="en">Physical Education</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Religion</div><div class="en">Religious Studies</div></div>
  <div class="vocab-card"><div class="de">Literatur</div><div class="en">Literature</div></div>
</div>
```

---

## Talking About Your Timetable {#sub-timetable}

| German | English |
|--------|---------|
| Am Montag habe ich zwei Stunden Religion. | On Monday I have two periods of Religion. |
| Wann hast du Mathematik? | When do you have Mathematics? |
| Am Montag in der ersten Stunde habe ich ___. | On Monday in the first period I have ___. |
| Am Freitag in der vierten Stunde habe ich frei. | On Friday in the fourth period I have a free period. |
| Jeden Tag habe ich ___. | Every day I have ___. |

### Ordinal Numbers for Periods

| Ordinal | German | English |
|---------|--------|---------|
| 1. | erste(n) | first |
| 2. | zweite(n) | second |
| 3. | dritte(n) | third |
| 4. | vierte(n) | fourth |
| 5. | fünfte(n) | fifth |
| 6. | sechste(n) | sixth |
| 7. | siebte(n) | seventh |
| 8. | achte(n) | eighth |

---

## Classroom Objects {#sub-classobj}

| German | Article | English | German | Article | English |
|--------|---------|---------|--------|---------|---------|
| Tafel | die | blackboard | Stift | der | pen/marker |
| Tasche | die | bag | Bleistift | der | pencil |
| Laptop | das | laptop | Radiergummi | der | eraser |
| Fenster | das | window | Tisch | der | table |
| Tür | die | door | Stuhl | der | chair |
| Kuli | der | ballpoint pen | Uhr | die | clock |
| Buch | das | book | Heft | das | notebook |
| Rucksack | der | backpack | Lineal | das | ruler |
| Computer | der | computer | Handy | das | mobile phone |

---

## Classroom Language {#sub-classlang}

| German | English |
|--------|---------|
| Lesen Sie bitte vor. | Please read aloud. |
| Noch einmal, bitte. | Once more, please. |
| Ergänzen Sie. | Fill in / Complete. |
| Wie heißt das auf Deutsch? | How do you say that in German? |
| Können Sie das wiederholen, bitte? | Can you repeat that, please? |
| Ich verstehe das nicht. | I don't understand that. |
| Ich brauche Hilfe. | I need help. |
| Was sind die Hausaufgaben? | What is the homework? |
| Wann ist die Pause? | When is the break? |
| Darf ich kurz auf die Toilette gehen? | May I quickly go to the toilet? |

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════ SECTION: Time & Calendar ═══════════════════════════ -->

::: {#sec-time .content-section}

```{=html}
<div class="topic-banner">
  <div><span class="de-article">die</span><span class="de-word">Zeit und der Kalender</span></div>
  <div class="pron-word">[tsyte oont dare kah-LEN-der]</div>
  <div class="en-word">Time and Calendar</div>
</div>
```

## Days of the Week {#sub-days}

::: {.callout-note}
Days of the week are all **masculine (der)**. Use without an article.  
To say **'on Monday'**: use **'am Montag'** (am = an + dem).
:::

| German | Pronunciation | English | Short |
|--------|---------------|---------|-------|
| (der) Montag | [MON-tahk] | Monday | Mo |
| (der) Dienstag | [DEENS-tahk] | Tuesday | Di |
| (der) Mittwoch | [MIT-vokh] | Wednesday | Mi |
| (der) Donnerstag | [DON-ners-tahk] | Thursday | Do |
| (der) Freitag | [FRY-tahk] | Friday | Fr |
| (der) Samstag | [ZAM-stahk] | Saturday | Sa |
| (der) Sonntag | [ZON-tahk] | Sunday | So |
| das Wochenende | [VO-khen-en-deh] | weekend | |

---

## Months {#sub-months}

::: {.callout-note}
Months are **masculine (der)**. Use **'im Januar'** to say 'in January'.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="num-grid">
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#93c5fd"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Jan</div><div class="word">Januar</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#93c5fd"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Feb</div><div class="word">Februar</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#86efac"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Mär</div><div class="word">März</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#86efac"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Apr</div><div class="word">April</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#86efac"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Mai</div><div class="word">Mai</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#fde047"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Jun</div><div class="word">Juni</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#fde047"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Jul</div><div class="word">Juli</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#fde047"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Aug</div><div class="word">August</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#fb923c"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Sep</div><div class="word">September</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#fb923c"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Okt</div><div class="word">Oktober</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#fb923c"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Nov</div><div class="word">November</div></div>
  <div class="num-card" style="border-color:#93c5fd"><div class="num" style="font-size:.95rem">Dez</div><div class="word">Dezember</div></div>
</div>
```

---

## Seasons {#sub-seasons}

```{=html}
<div class="season-grid">
  <div class="season-card winter"><div class="icon">❄️</div><div class="name">der Winter</div><div class="eng">winter</div><div class="months">Dez · Jan · Feb</div></div>
  <div class="season-card spring"><div class="icon">🌸</div><div class="name">der Frühling</div><div class="eng">spring</div><div class="months">Mär · Apr · Mai</div></div>
  <div class="season-card summer"><div class="icon">☀️</div><div class="name">der Sommer</div><div class="eng">summer</div><div class="months">Jun · Jul · Aug</div></div>
  <div class="season-card autumn"><div class="icon">🍂</div><div class="name">der Herbst</div><div class="eng">autumn / fall</div><div class="months">Sep · Okt · Nov</div></div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-tip}
All four seasons are **masculine (der)**. Use **'im Sommer'**, **'im Winter'** etc.  
**'In welchem Monat hast du Geburtstag?'** = In which month is your birthday?
:::

---

## Useful Time Expressions {#sub-timeexpr}

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| heute | [HOY-teh] | today |
| morgen | [MOR-gen] | tomorrow |
| übermorgen | [UE-ber-mor-gen] | day after tomorrow |
| gestern | [GES-tern] | yesterday |
| vorgestern | [FOR-ges-tern] | day before yesterday |
| diese Woche | [DEE-zeh VO-kheh] | this week |
| nächste Woche | [NEKH-steh VO-kheh] | next week |
| letzte Woche | [LETS-teh VO-kheh] | last week |
| am Montag | [am MON-tahk] | on Monday |
| im Januar | [im YAH-noo-ar] | in January |

---

## Telling the Time {#sub-telling}

### Asking the Time

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| Wie spät ist es? | [vee shpayt ist ess] | What time is it? |
| Um wie viel Uhr ist es jetzt? | [oom vee feel oor ist ess yetst] | At what time is it now? |
| Es ist ___. | [ess ist] | It is ___. |

### Official Time (24-hour clock)

| Time | German | English |
|------|--------|---------|
| 8:00 | Es ist acht Uhr. | It is 8 o'clock. |
| 8:05 | Es ist acht Uhr fünf. | It is 8:05. |
| 8:15 | Es ist acht Uhr fünfzehn. | It is 8:15. |
| 8:30 | Es ist acht Uhr dreißig. | It is 8:30. |
| 13:00 | Es ist dreizehn Uhr. | It is 1pm. |

### Informal Time (12-hour with words)

::: {.callout-warning}
**⚠️ CRITICAL:** **'halb acht'** = **7:30**, NOT 8:30!  
It means *'half [on the way to] eight'*.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="clock-row">
  <div class="clock-item"><div class="time">7:30</div><div class="de">halb acht</div><div class="en">half [to] eight → 7:30!</div></div>
  <div class="clock-item"><div class="time">8:15</div><div class="de">Viertel nach acht</div><div class="en">quarter past eight</div></div>
  <div class="clock-item"><div class="time">8:45</div><div class="de">Viertel vor neun</div><div class="en">quarter to nine</div></div>
  <div class="clock-item"><div class="time">8:05</div><div class="de">fünf nach acht</div><div class="en">five past eight</div></div>
  <div class="clock-item"><div class="time">8:55</div><div class="de">fünf vor neun</div><div class="en">five to nine</div></div>
</div>
```

### Time Periods of the Day

| German | Pronunciation | English |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| der Morgen / früh | [MOR-gen / frue] | morning |
| der Vormittag | [FOR-mit-tahk] | before noon |
| der Mittag | [MIT-tahk] | noon |
| der Nachmittag | [NAHKH-mit-tahk] | afternoon |
| der Abend | [AH-bent] | evening |
| die Nacht | [nakht] | night |
| die Mitternacht | [MIT-ter-nakht] | midnight |

:::

```{=html}
<div style="text-align:center;margin-top:3rem;padding:1.5rem;color:#9333ea;font-size:.9rem;">
  🎉 <strong>Herzlichen Glückwunsch</strong> — you've reviewed all sections! Keep practising every day. <em>Viel Erfolg!</em> (Good luck!)
</div>
</div><!-- /main-content -->
```
